full_text_rationale: Full text describes AgentGA launching fresh autonomous code-generation runs with inherited parent archives that persist reusable artifacts across generations, with Weco-Kaggle Lite and parent-child lineage evaluation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 007; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: David Y. Y. Tan, Kellie Chin, Jingxian Zhang
titulo: AgentGA: Evolving Code Solutions in Agent-Seed Space
formato_armazenamento: Population-level archive of autonomous run artifacts, parent solutions, experiments, agent chains, and code artifacts; selected parent archives are copied into a Previous Experiments directory for child runs while each child starts from a fresh workspace.
framework_agente: AGENT GA agent-seed genetic algorithm with LangGraph-based autonomous code-generation runs
benchmarks_utilizados: 10 of 16 Weco-Kaggle Lite tabular AutoML competitions, Kaggle private leaderboard submissions, 1,135 parent-child tournaments
metricas_desempenho: Exceeds percent of human competitors, Kaggle rank, parent-child tournament win/loss share, median relative gain, prompt/completion/cached token trace for example run
baseline_comparado: AIDE benchmark reference; contextual comparisons to H2O AutoML, AutoGPT, and human with ChatGPT; Initial de novo proposals versus parent-conditioned operators
tokens_entrada_por_tarefa: Example invocation consumed 1.87M prompt tokens including 1.56M cached prompt tokens.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Archive persists every iteration, run, agent chain, parent solution, and experiment artifact across generations; operators choose zero, one, or two parent archives plus optional reference archives.
custo_por_tarefa: No monetary cost reported; example invocation took 56 steps, 15 min 10 s, 1.87M prompt tokens, and 34,252 completion tokens.
resultado_principal: Across 10 completed Weco-Kaggle Lite benchmark runs, AGENT GA averages 74.52% Exceeds % of Human versus 54.15% for AIDE, a +20.36 point mean improvement. All 10 completed runs beat the AIDE private-leaderboard reference.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Parent-conditioned operators win 48.7% of parent-child tournaments overall, while Initial proposals win only 12.2%. Continue wins 148/286 tournaments, Merge 68/104, EDA 267/593, Ablation 34/78, and Initial 9/74.
custo_vs_baseline: Long-horizon agents consume substantially more tokens and wall-clock time than artifact-centric generation; no direct monetary baseline comparison is reported.
limitacoes: Only 10 of 16 benchmark runs are reported; long-horizon compute cost is high; seed quality is observed only indirectly through stochastic descendants; parent archives are optional context and can be consumed inconsistently.
ameacas_validade: Partial benchmark coverage, stochastic trajectories, Kaggle task/domain focus, multi-split evaluation choices, tournament operator allocation, and unpublished full artifacts may affect validity.
